highlights.description: Project renamed from MCPKit to OmniSkill
New `skill/` package with unified skill infrastructure for AI agents
Registry package for skill and tool management

upgradeGuide.description: Run `go mod tidy` to update dependencies

added.description: `skill.Skill` interface defining `Name()`, `Description()`, `Tools()`, `Init()`, and `Close()` methods
`skill.Tool` interface for callable tools with `Name()`, `Description()`, `Parameters()`, and `Call()` methods
`skill.FuncTool` struct implementing `Tool` interface with function handler
`skill.NewTool()` constructor for creating tools with parameters and handlers
`skill.Parameter` type with JSON Schema-compatible fields (Type, Description, Required, Default, Enum, Items, Properties)
`skill.BaseSkill` helper struct for simple skill implementations
`skill.ParametersToJSONSchema()` for converting parameters to JSON Schema format
`skill.ToJSONSchema()` for generating complete tool JSON Schema for LLM function calling
`registry.Registry` for managing skills and discovering tools
`registry.Register()` and `registry.Unregister()` for skill lifecycle management
`registry.Get()`, `registry.List()`, `registry.Count()` for skill access
`registry.GetTool()`, `registry.ListTools()`, `registry.FindTool()` for tool discovery
`registry.Init()` and `registry.Close()` for batch skill lifecycle
`mcp/runtime.RegisterSkill()` for exposing skill tools as MCP tools
`mcp/runtime.RegisterRegistry()` for auto-registering all skills from a registry
`mcp/client.Session.AsSkill()` for wrapping MCP sessions as skills

documentation.description: Concepts documentation for skills, tools, and registry

highlights.description: MCP client package for connecting to external MCP servers

added.description: `client/` package for MCP client connections
`client.New()` constructor with configurable client name and version
`client.Connect()` for establishing sessions with any MCP transport
`client.ConnectCommand()` for spawning MCP servers as subprocesses
`Session.ListTools()` for discovering available tools from MCP server
`Session.CallTool()` for invoking tools on MCP server
`Session.MCPSession()` escape hatch for advanced MCP SDK access

highlights.description: Organization renamed from agentplexus to plexusone
Changelog format updated to camelCase for ecosystem consistency

upgradeGuide.description: Run `go mod tidy` to update dependencies

changed.description: Documentation and badge URLs updated to plexusone organization
Changelog JSON format migrated from snake_case to camelCase

highlights.description: Security fix for open URL redirect vulnerability in OAuth2 server

security.description: Close open URL redirect vulnerability in `isValidRedirectURI` - wildcards no longer allow arbitrary redirect URIs

fixed.description: OAuth2 redirect URI validation now properly parses and validates URIs structurally

highlights.description: Project renamed to MCPKit under github.com/plexusone/mcpkit
Improved error handling following Go best practices

changed.description: Core runtime code moved to `runtime/` subpackage - import `github.com/plexusone/mcpkit/runtime`
OAuth2 server moved from `oauth2server/` to `oauth2/` - import `github.com/plexusone/mcpkit/oauth2`
Package references changed from `mcpkit.` to `runtime.` for runtime types

fixed.description: Error handling for `fmt.Fprintf` in OAuth login error page
Error handling for `resp.Body.Close()` in all test files

breaking.description: Package name changed: update `mcpruntime.` or `mcpkit.` references to `runtime.`
OAuth2 server import path changed: update `oauth2server` to `github.com/plexusone/mcpkit/oauth2`

highlights.description: OAuth 2.1 Authorization Code + PKCE support for ChatGPT.com and other MCP clients requiring user authentication
HTTP server mode with optional ngrok tunnel for public URL exposure
Bearer token authentication for protecting MCP endpoints

added.description: `ServeHTTP()` method for HTTP transport with graceful shutdown
`NgrokOptions` for automatic ngrok tunnel creation with custom domain support
`OAuth2Options` for OAuth 2.1 Authorization Code + PKCE authentication
`oauth2server` package implementing RFC 7636 (PKCE), RFC 7591 (DCR), RFC 8414, RFC 9728
Bearer token authentication middleware for protected MCP endpoints
`HTTPServerResult` with local and public URLs plus OAuth credentials
`OnReady` callback in `HTTPServerOptions` for server lifecycle events

deprecated.description: `OAuthOptions` (client_credentials only) in favor of `OAuth2Options` with full OAuth 2.1 PKCE support

highlights.description: Library-first MCP runtime: define tools, prompts, and resources once, invoke them directly as Go functions or expose over MCP transports
Zero JSON-RPC overhead in library mode for unit testing, embedding, and serverless use cases
Full MCP SDK compatibility with escape hatch for advanced features

added.description: `Runtime` type wrapping `mcp.Server` with dual execution modes (library and server)
`New()` constructor with `Options` for runtime configuration
`MCPServer()` escape hatch for advanced MCP SDK access
`Implementation()` getter for server identity
`AddTool[In, Out]()` generic helper with automatic schema inference
`AddToolHandler()` low-level tool registration
`CallTool()` for library-mode direct invocation
`RemoveTools()`, `ListTools()`, `HasTool()`, `ToolCount()` tool management methods
`AddPrompt()` for prompt registration
`GetPrompt()` for library-mode direct invocation
`RemovePrompts()`, `ListPrompts()`, `HasPrompt()`, `PromptCount()` prompt management methods
`AddResource()` for static resource registration
`AddResourceTemplate()` for dynamic URI template resources (server mode only)
`ReadResource()` for library-mode direct invocation
`RemoveResources()`, `RemoveResourceTemplates()` resource removal methods
`ListResources()`, `HasResource()`, `ResourceCount()` resource management methods
`ServeStdio()` for stdio transport (subprocess mode)
`ServeIO()` for custom IO streams
`Serve()` for custom MCP transports
`Connect()` for session-based connections
`StreamableHTTPHandler()` for HTTP/SSE transport
`SSEHandler()` for legacy SSE transport
`InMemorySession()` for testing with full MCP semantics

documentation.description: Comprehensive README with library and server mode examples
Feature comparison matrix (library vs server mode)
